title: Cox Farms
description: Working farm with seasonal festivals, corn maze, slides, hayrides, and farm animals. Famous for the Fall Festival and Fields of Fear Halloween event.
parentHacks: The big slides are worth the wait - adults can go too!
Go on a weekday if possible - weekends are packed
Wear shoes you don't mind getting muddy
The corn maze has an easy path marked for little ones

title: Fairfax Ice Arena
description: Year-round ice skating facility offering public skating sessions, hockey leagues, and figure skating classes. Skate rentals available. Check schedule for public session times.
parentHacks: Call ahead to confirm public skate times - schedule changes often
Rental skates are included in admission
Bring thick socks - rental skates can be cold and uncomfortable
The snack bar has decent hot chocolate

title: Go Ape Zipline & Adventure Park
description: Outdoor treetop adventure course featuring ziplines, rope ladders, Tarzan swings, and obstacles. Two courses available: one for adults/teens and a junior course for ages 5+.

schedule.seasonalNotes: Weekends only in spring/fall. Closed Dec-Feb.

parentHacks: The junior course is perfect for kids 5-12 and includes supervision
Wear closed-toe shoes - no sandals allowed
Book weeks in advance for weekends
Bring layers - it's cooler up in the trees

title: Great Country Farms
description: Family farm offering seasonal pick-your-own produce, farm animals, hayrides, and a massive playground. Special events throughout the year including pumpkin patches and sunflower festivals.
parentHacks: The giant slides are worth the trek up the hill
Bring a wagon for hauling produce and tired toddlers
Arrive when they open to beat the crowds
Pack snacks - food options are limited and lines get long

title: KidScience Labs Fairfax
description: Hands-on science enrichment center offering classes, camps, and drop-in lab sessions. Kids conduct real experiments in chemistry, physics, biology, and engineering.
parentHacks: Drop-in sessions fill up fast - call ahead to reserve a spot
Kids get to take home what they make/create
Great option for birthday parties
Wear clothes that can get messy

title: Launch Trampoline Park
description: Indoor trampoline park featuring wall-to-wall trampolines, foam pits, dodgeball courts, ninja warrior courses, and a dedicated area for younger jumpers. Perfect for burning energy on rainy days.
parentHacks: Buy the special grip socks online beforehand - cheaper than at the door
Weekday mornings are much less crowded
The toddler area is separate and safe from big kids
Bring water bottles - they have fill stations

title: LEGO Discovery Center
description: Interactive LEGO attraction featuring rides, 4D cinema, building workshops, and millions of LEGO bricks. Designed for families with children ages 3-10.
parentHacks: Book online in advance for the best price - walk-up is expensive
Adults must be accompanied by a child (no solo adult visits)
The 4D cinema is loud - might scare sensitive little ones
2-3 hours is plenty of time for most families

title: Mosaic District Playground & Splash Pad
description: Modern outdoor playground and seasonal splash pad in the heart of Mosaic District. Surrounded by restaurants and shops. Free to enjoy.
parentHacks: Parking garage validates first 2 hours free with purchase
Ted's Bulletin across the street has great brunch
Bring a change of clothes for splash pad days
The playground has good shade coverage

title: National Museum of Natural History
description: World-renowned museum featuring dinosaur fossils, the Hope Diamond, live butterfly pavilion, and hands-on discovery rooms. Free admission makes it perfect for repeat visits.
parentHacks: The Butterfly Pavilion has timed tickets - get them first thing
Q?rius on the ground floor is perfect for hands-on exploration
Take the Metro - parking nearby is expensive and limited
Download the museum app for kid-friendly scavenger hunts

title: Reston Zoo
description: Small family-friendly zoo with farm animals, exotic species, pony rides, and a petting area. Educational programs and birthday party options available.
parentHacks: Pony rides are extra but the kids love them
Small enough to see everything in 1.5-2 hours
The picnic area is nice for packed lunches
Not much shade - bring hats and sunscreen in summer

title: Roer's Zoofari
description: Family-owned zoo featuring exotic animals from around the world. Get up close with giraffes, zebras, and lemurs. Feed animals and enjoy the petting zoo area.
parentHacks: Buy animal feed at the entrance - kids love feeding the animals
The giraffe feeding is a must-do experience
Weekday mornings are much quieter
Mostly flat paths - stroller friendly

title: Steven F. Udvar-Hazy Center
description: Explore the companion facility to the National Air and Space Museum featuring thousands of aviation and space artifacts including the Space Shuttle Discovery, a Lockheed SR-71 Blackbird, and the Boeing B-29 Superfortress Enola Gay.
parentHacks: Free parking lot fills up by 11am on weekends - arrive early
The flight simulators are extra cost but worth it for older kids
Pack a lunch - the cafe is pricey and limited

title: Zava Zone
description: Multi-level indoor playground designed for kids 12 and under. Features climbing structures, slides, ball pits, and a separate toddler area. Great for birthday parties and play dates.
parentHacks: Adults don't pay if they're not playing - just supervising
Socks required - they sell them if you forget
The toddler section is perfect for little ones away from big kids
Check for Groupon deals before you go

title: Culmore Library Outreach: Play Date
description: Culmore United Methodist Church Events at Other Locations Drop in for stories, songs, and playtime. Make new friends during self-guided play. No registration or tickets. Doors close when room capacity is reached. For babies to age 5 with caregiver. This event is held offsite at: Culmore Church 3400 Charles St, Falls Church, VA 22041

title: Learn to Ride
description: Registration required. resenter: Andrew Sheldon Oakton Lobby Oakton Library Have you ever wanted to learn to ride a bicycle? Here is your chance! Fairfax County Department of Transportation and Oakton Library offer a children's bike education class to teach the skills to incorporate bicycling into your daily routine–whether that’s for fitness, fun, or transportation. If you have a bicycle and helmet, please bring them to the class. If you are in need of a helmet or bicycle, please re

title: Little Wiggles Storytime
description: Registration required. resenter: Kyung Yoo Chantilly Meeting Room Chantilly Regional Library Join us to have fun together with stories, songs, fingerplays, lap bounces, books and social time for children from birth to 20 months!

title: Baby and Me Storytime
description: Registration required. Lorton Meeting Room Lorton Library Storytime for Ages Birth-18 months Join us for rhymes, songs, stories and activities for you and your baby. Build an early literacy foundation while enjoying time together Registration Registration required for baby only. Siblings welcome.

title: Baby Rhyme Time
description: Registration required. Martha Washington Meeting Room 104A , Martha Washington Meeting Room 104B Martha Washington Library This lapsit story and song time is for the youngest patrons and their caregivers. Age birth - 17 months with adult. Please register children only.

title: Bouncin' Babies Storytime
description: resenter: Andréa F. George Mason Meeting Room George Mason Regional Library Bond with your baby while you enjoy short stories, songs and lapsit activities in this interactive storytime. Ages For children birth - 18 months with adult.

title: Preschool Time
description: resenter: Kate Burke Centre Library Stories, songs, and activities that lay the foundation your child needs to get ready to read! For children ages 3-5 with caregiver.

title: Toddlin' Time at Tysons
description: Tysons-Pimmit Meeting Room 1, Tysons-Pimmit Meeting Room 2 Tysons-Pimmit Regional Library Join us for stories and songs as your child moves and grooves with other toddlers. Build an early literacy foundation while enjoying time together in your lap. Meets in person at Tysons Pimmit Library. Space limited to 35 toddlers with one caregiver each. No registration. Entrance tickets are provided first come, first served at the Information Desk 30 minutes before the program begins. For toddlers ages 1.

title: Bilingual (Spanish-English) Storytime
description: resenter: City of Fairfax Regional Library Staff City of Fairfax Meeting Room 101A, City of Fairfax Meeting Room 101B City of Fairfax Regional Library Hola amigos! Hi Friends! Do you enjoy singing and dancing to silly songs and listening to stories in Spanish and English? Ages 0 – 5 ¿Te encantas cantar y bailar con canciones divertidas y escuchar cuentos en español e inglés? Edades 0 - 5 Registration No registration No es necesario registrarse.

title: Princess Story Time
description: Kings Park Meeting Room Kings Park Library Princess Belle visits the Kings Park Library in this magical, interactive storytime. Come hear stories, dance to music, and get a photo with your favorite book-loving princess! Ages 0-5. No Registration required. Tickets will be handed out at the Information Desk 20 minutes prior to event. Limit 20 families.

title: Toddlers & Twos Storytime at Herndon Library
description: resenter: Kathleen McCarthy Herndon Fortnightly Meeting Room Herndon Fortnightly Library Enjoy an early literacy storytime geared towards toddlers, with stories, songs and fingerplays. Ages 16-36 months with adult. No registration. Space is limited. Please arrive early. Late arrivals are not guaranteed admission. --- Tips for a Successful Storytime Sit, sing, & listen to stories together. The more you participate in storytime, the more children will participate, enjoy, and learn from storytime.

title: The Goodlife Theater Presents: Grandpa's Farm Puppet Show
description: Centreville Meeting Room 1, Centreville Meeting Room 2 Centreville Regional Library Take an imaginary journey to Grandpa's Farm. Join Overalls the farm dog as you meet interesting characters like Sunny Sunshine, Farmer Fowler, and some garden friends and are guided through how to plant a vegetable garden! For children ages 3+ and families. No registration required. Space is limited. Tickets will be given out 30 mins before the show. All parties must be present to receive a ticket. First come, fi

title: Bike Safety Rodeo & Trail Ride
description: Registration required. resenter: Andrew Sheldon Oakton Lobby Oakton Library Rustle up your bikes and helmets for Oakton's bike safety rodeo! Learn how to ride safely with an interactive skills course. For school age kids ages 6-12. The rodeo is a fun way for kids to build their biking skills and develop a greater understanding of bike safety. Try out the interactive and non-competitive skills course designed for kids to improve their bike handling and safety skills. Please bring your own bike an

title: Teddy Bear Picnic
description: Registration required. Sherwood Meeting Room Sherwood Regional Library You and your favorite stuffed animal are invited to a picnic. We will have a craft, snacks, and bubbles! The event will be held outdoors weather-permitting. PLEASE bring a towel or blanket to sit on the grass! For ages 18 months through 5 years. Registration is not required, but sign-up below to receive reminders about the event.

title: Wee Bilingual Korean-English Storytime
description: Registration required. resenter: Kyung Yoo, Information Chantilly Meeting Room Chantilly Regional Library Join us to have fun together with songs, fingerplays, dances, and stories in both Korean and English. For families with children ages 2 to 5 years. **Please register each child separately. There is no need to register adults who will be attending.

title: LEGO Club
description: Burke Centre Meeting Room 116, Burke Centre Meeting Room 117 Burke Centre Library Calling all LEGO lovers! Drop in after school to create and build with LEGO bricks. Meet others who love building creations as much as you! Ages 6-12.

title: LEGO Club
description: Tysons-Pimmit Meeting Room 1, Tysons-Pimmit Meeting Room 2 Tysons-Pimmit Regional Library Make new friends and Lego creations! Collaboration and friendly competition is encouraged! All Legos are provided and stay at the library. This is for ages 6-12 years old with one parent. Space is limited to 25 kids. Tickets will be distributed on a first-come, first-served basis, starting 30 minutes before the program begins. If you have questions about the appropriate age for a program or its content plea

title: Spring Bingo
description: resenter: Marcia and Anita Oakton Meeting Room 1, Oakton Meeting Room 2 Oakton Library Join us for Spring Bingo! Have fun with the whole family playing various rounds of Spring-themed Bingo Games. Simple prizes for everyone! No registration or tickets required. Doors will close when room capacity is reached and/or supplies are gone.

title: Spring Break Movie Matinee
description: Martha Washington Meeting Room 104A , Martha Washington Meeting Room 104B Martha Washington Library Bring the whole family for a movie at the library. We'll be showing “Zootopia 2" rated "PG"

title: Science for Kids - Kaleidoscopes
description: Registration required. City of Fairfax Meeting Room 101A, City of Fairfax Meeting Room 101B City of Fairfax Regional Library Student presenters from Technology Youth Empowerment will lead a class introducing optics and kaleidoscopes. You will make your own kaleidoscope to take home! This class is designed for kids ages 9-12 years old. Registration Pre-registration is required. Please register each child, and only children in the correct age range (9-12). Please note late arrivals will not be adm

title: Spring Break-Movie and Craft Day
description: Registration required. Lorton Meeting Room Lorton Library Enjoy a little Spring Break down time with a family-friendly movie, "The Lorax" and create some Truffula Trees to take home with you. Ages 0-12 years. Registration Required for all attending.

title: Infinite Legacy Bingo
description: Registration required. resenter: Marta Steenstra Davis Dolley Madison Library Join us as we partner with Infinite Legacy for Donate Life Month! They will present a fun and educational bingo game to learn about organ and tissue donation! There will be prizes!

title: Spring Break Pop-Up LEGO Club
description: Pohick Meeting Room 1, Pohick Meeting Room 2 Pohick Regional Library Calling all LEGO lovers! Celebrate spring break by building your own LEGO masterpieces. Ages 5 & up. No Registration required.

title: Minecraft with Arrayscape Gaming
description: Registration required. resenter: volunteer Great Falls Meeting Room Great Falls Library Island Survival Test Embark on a deserted tropical island challenge where you'll learn to gather resources, hunt for food, and fend off wild creatures. Build shelter, explore the village for valuable loot, and navigate through mines using parkour skills to find hidden treasures. With no boats available, venture into mysterious caves to conquer the island and build your base! *Registration Required* Cosponsore

title: LEGO Construction Zone
description: Centreville Meeting Room 1, Centreville Meeting Room 2 Centreville Regional Library Calling all brick-building enthusiasts! We provide the LEGO - you provide the creativity! What will you decide to build? This program is intended for children in grades 1-6. No registration required.

description: Hone your birding skills on the trail and learn to play our favorite…

title: Spring Into Action: All About Owls Campfire
description: Barred owls inhabit our stream valley parks. Learn to identify these…

title: Easter Eggstravaganza
description: The biggest Easter egg hunt in Northern Virginia! Over 20,000 eggs hidden across the farm. Meet the Easter Bunny, enjoy hayrides, baby animal cuddles, and spring activities. Egg hunts divided by age group.
parentHacks: Bring your own basket - they don't provide them
Check the egg hunt schedule by age group on arrival
The baby animals are the real stars
Book online in advance to guarantee entry

title: Strawberry Jubilee
description: Pick your own fresh strawberries at the height of the season! Enjoy strawberry-themed treats, live music, farm animals, and playground fun. Strawberry picking included with admission.
parentHacks: Go early - the best berries get picked by noon
Bring sunscreen - not much shade in the fields
The strawberry shortcake is worth the extra cost

title: Sunflower Festival
description: Walk through acres of stunning sunflowers in full bloom! Cut your own sunflowers to take home, enjoy photo ops, hayrides, and summer farm activities. Perfect for family photos.
parentHacks: Early morning or golden hour for the best photos
Bring scissors or garden shears for cutting sunflowers
The fields are HOT in August - bring water and hats

title: Pumpkin Harvest Festival
description: The ultimate fall family experience! Pick your own pumpkins, navigate the corn maze, enjoy hayrides, apple cider donuts, and dozens of farm activities. Don't miss the giant slides and jumping pillows!
parentHacks: Buy tickets online to skip the line
Pumpkins are pay-by-the-pound in addition to admission
Bring a wagon to haul pumpkins and tired kids
The corn maze has an easy route marked for young kids
